A sealed glyph machine transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs
by one fixed hidden rule.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

7. drop before zim

E3: ulm tosk zim fole ulm gav tosk ulm zim => zim tosk tosk gav zim tosk | shown wheel: match
E4: zim zim ulm gav ulm sabb zim nack ulm => zim tosk nack zim sabb sabb tosk gav zim | shown wheel gives zim tosk zim sabb sabb tosk gav

Seven rune wards seal a vault door: ule, kelm, sorv, quen, brasp, mott, osk.
Each ward holds one whole-number charge from 1 to 8. Charges may
repeat unless a binding forbids it. The warding script declares:

1. The charge of mott is an odd number.
2. The sorv charge is exactly 2 more than the quen charge.
3. The sorv ward holds more than the quen ward.
4. Together, ule, sorv, and osk hold exactly 10.
5. The kelm charge is exactly 2 more than the quen charge.
6. The kelm and mott wards hold different charges.
7. The kelm, quen, and brasp charges add up to exactly 8.
8. Together, mott and osk hold exactly 9.
9. The osk ward holds an even charge.
10. The sorv charge is exactly 2 more than the ule charge.
11. The quen and mott wards hold different charges.
12. The ule, kelm, and brasp charges add up to exactly 8.
13. The mott ward holds more than the sorv ward.

Either the bindings force a single possible assignment of charges,
or they contradict one another and cannot all hold at once.
If they force one assignment, give the charge of the ule
ward. If they contradict, give exactly the word IMPOSSIBLE.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:

rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. rotate left
2. swap first last
3. drop after ruv
4. rotate left
5. rotate left
6. reverse all

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):
E1: gav ulm tosk ulm gav gav gav => tosk ulm gav gav gav ulm gav | shown wheel gives tosk gav ulm gav gav gav ulm
E2: ruv tosk ruv ruv sabb ulm yeg => ruv ulm yeg tosk ruv | shown wheel gives ruv ruv tosk yeg ulm
E3: ulm gav ruv ruv gav yeg tosk => ruv gav yeg tosk gav ulm | shown wheel gives ruv ulm gav tosk yeg gav

Work out the corrupted rule from the evidence, repair it, then
apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: yeg sabb sabb ruv ulm yeg yeg
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

This SPOOL routine was correct until exactly one line was changed
(the planted bug):

1: ADD A 3
2: IFZ C
3: SUB D 5
4: COPY C B
5: ZERO D

Each test starts the registers as shown; 'correct' is what the
original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

T1: start A=5 B=0 C=4 D=0 | correct A=8 B=0 C=0 D=0 | buggy A=8 B=0 C=0 D=0 -> pass
T2: start A=3 B=0 C=5 D=8 | correct A=6 B=0 C=0 D=3 | buggy A=6 B=0 C=0 D=0 -> FAIL

Which line number holds the planted bug? (If replacing some other
single line would also make every test pass, that line number is
accepted too.)

- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)

The 12 steps:
- Coiling: needs Sealing, Slagturn, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=LOWERED; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT.

- Tamping: needs Cold Turning, Quenching, ash-vent=BOUND; then damper=SHUT.

- Charmark: needs Quenching, Scrying, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then damper=SHUT, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- First Draw: needs Scrying, Sealing, Slagturn, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET.
- Veiling: needs Coiling, Cold Turning, Slagturn, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then soot-gate=RAISED, fire-core=COLD.
- Kindlecall: needs Veiling, damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT.
- Cold Turning: needs Scrying, ash-vent=BOUND, damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET; then ash-vent=FREE, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Scrying: needs soot-gate=RAISED; then glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Ashdraw: needs Kindlecall, Quenching, Slagturn, ash-vent=BOUND, soot-gate=RAISED; then slip-ring=WHOLE, ash-vent=FREE.
- Sealing: needs Cold Turning, Scrying, Slagturn, ash-vent=BOUND, damper=SHUT; then fire-core=LIT.

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 12 steps:
- Glossing: needs Charmark, Cold Turning, First Draw, glaze-mark=UNSET; then soot-gate=RAISED, fire-core=LIT.
- Emberfast: needs Charmark, First Draw, Raking, ash-vent=FREE; then fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Veiling: needs glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=RAISED; then slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Wadding: needs Raking, Tamping, Veiling, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET.
- Quenching: needs Coiling, Tamping, Wadding, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=COLD.
- Raking: needs Kindlecall, Tamping, slip-ring=WHOLE; then damper=SHUT, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Coiling: needs Veiling, ash-vent=BOUND, damper=SHUT, soot-gate=RAISED; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT.
- Kindlecall: needs Veiling, damper=OPEN, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=FREE, soot-gate=RAISED.
- First Draw: needs Veiling, Wadding, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=LIT.
- Cold Turning: needs Wadding, ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED; then ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=COLD.
- Charmark: needs Raking, ash-vent=BOUND, soot-gate=RAISED; then glaze-mark=UNSET, ash-vent=FREE.
- Tamping: needs Veiling, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

Steps done so far, in order: Veiling, Kindlecall, Tamping, Raking, Coiling, Wadding, Charmark, First Draw, Quenching.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. every tosk becomes hib
2. rotate left
3. every hib becomes zim
4. drop after tosk

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):
E1: dree tosk dree zim dree => dree zim dree zim dree | shown wheel gives zim dree zim dree dree
E2: tosk dree zim hib dree => dree zim zim dree zim | shown wheel: match

Work out the corrupted rule from the evidence, repair it, then
apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: hib fole zim fole fole
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 10 steps:
- Tamping: needs Raking, Slagturn, damper=OPEN, fire-core=COLD; then damper=SHUT.
- Cold Turning: needs Glossing, Slagturn, slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD.
- Glossing: needs Slagturn, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=LIT.
- Veiling: needs Cold Turning, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE; then fire-core=LIT.
- Slagturn: needs damper=SHUT, slip-ring=CRACKED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Quenching: needs Glossing, Slagturn, damper=SHUT, fire-core=LIT; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- First Draw: needs Quenching, Slagturn, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD, damper=OPEN.
- Raking: needs Cold Turning, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=RAISED, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Ashdraw: needs Slagturn, Veiling, ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=COLD; then damper=SHUT.
- Charmark: needs Quenching, ash-vent=FREE, slip-ring=WHOLE; then soot-gate=RAISED, damper=OPEN.

Steps done so far, in order: Slagturn, Glossing, Quenching, Charmark, Cold Turning, Raking, Tamping, Veiling.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 10 steps done. Turn budget: 18.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)

The 10 steps:
- Wadding: needs ash-vent=BOUND, damper=SHUT; then slip-ring=CRACKED, damper=OPEN.
- Sealing: needs Wadding, glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=OPEN, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Quenching: needs Glossing, Wadding, slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=LOWERED; then damper=SHUT.
- Cold Turning: needs Wadding, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=CRACKED; then slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- First Draw: needs Cold Turning, glaze-mark=UNSET; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Raking: needs Kindlecall, ash-vent=BOUND; then glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Slagturn: needs Cold Turning, Scrying, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then soot-gate=RAISED, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Scrying: needs First Draw, slip-ring=WHOLE; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Glossing: needs Wadding, ash-vent=BOUND; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Kindlecall: needs Glossing, Wadding, ash-vent=FREE, soot-gate=LOWERED; then glaze-mark=UNSET.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end

drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A

drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before

double A = every A turns into A A

initial_observation: TARGET: fen-lus-lus-za-za-ti-fen. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: za-fen-gor-ro-ke. The gate accepts strings of exactly 5 glyphs. Probes left: 5. Turns left: 10.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 10 steps done. Turn budget: 18.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)

The 10 steps:
- Sealing: needs Raking, Wadding, fire-core=COLD; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Quenching: needs Ashdraw, Veiling, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=COLD.
- Raking: needs Glossing, Veiling, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=LOWERED, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Wadding: needs Veiling, ash-vent=BOUND; then soot-gate=RAISED.
- Ashdraw: needs Veiling, soot-gate=LOWERED; then soot-gate=RAISED, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Charmark: needs Ashdraw, Veiling, glaze-mark=UNSET; then soot-gate=LOWERED, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Coiling: needs Ashdraw, soot-gate=LOWERED; then ash-vent=FREE, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Glossing: needs Veiling, glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=RAISED; then glaze-mark=SET, fire-core=COLD.
- Cold Turning: needs Charmark, Wadding, fire-core=COLD; then fire-core=LIT, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Veiling: needs glaze-mark=SET; then ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: lus-gor-za-fen-fen-ro-fen. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 7 steps done. Turn budget: 10.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 7 steps:
- Emberfast: needs slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=LOWERED; then damper=OPEN, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Glossing: needs slip-ring=WHOLE; then slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Cold Turning: needs slip-ring=CRACKED; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Scrying: needs damper=SHUT, soot-gate=LOWERED; then damper=OPEN, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Charmark: needs slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=LOWERED; then slip-ring=WHOLE, damper=SHUT.
- Raking: needs soot-gate=RAISED; then slip-ring=CRACKED, damper=SHUT.
- Ashdraw: needs soot-gate=RAISED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: ti-mu-lus-gor-mu-ti-fen. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch

initial_observation: TARGET: gor-mu-gor-ti-ke-fen-ro. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 10 steps done. Turn budget: 18.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)

The 10 steps:
- Veiling: needs Emberfast, First Draw, soot-gate=LOWERED; then soot-gate=RAISED.
- Emberfast: needs Cold Turning, First Draw, damper=SHUT; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Tamping: needs Coiling, damper=SHUT; then glaze-mark=SET, fire-core=COLD.
- Charmark: needs First Draw, glaze-mark=SET; then fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Glossing: needs First Draw, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD.
- Coiling: needs glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=SHUT, fire-core=LIT.
- Cold Turning: needs First Draw, Slagturn, fire-core=COLD; then glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Kindlecall: needs Coiling, First Draw, fire-core=LIT; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Slagturn: needs Coiling, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- First Draw: needs Coiling, soot-gate=LOWERED; then soot-gate=RAISED.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: lus-ro-lus-za-za. The gate accepts strings of exactly 5 glyphs. Probes left: 5. Turns left: 10.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 7 steps done. Turn budget: 10.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)

The 7 steps:
- Quenching: needs Charmark, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Charmark: needs damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Emberfast: needs Tamping, glaze-mark=SET; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Wadding: needs damper=OPEN, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=COLD.
- Glossing: needs fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=UNSET; then glaze-mark=SET.
- Cold Turning: needs glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT.
- Tamping: needs Glossing, Wadding, damper=OPEN; then damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.
